school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: Either Ames goes with 84% or Dunn goes with 74%.
Hale's grade is lower than both the Geography class' grade and the class with Kira's grade.
Biology goes with exactly two of Dunn, Piper, 84%, and Period 1.
Period 3 goes with exactly two of Ames, Chemistry, Noah, and 79%.
English goes with either Carver or Hale.
Of the class with Piper and the Chemistry class, whoever has the higher grade has the higher period.
Period 5 goes with at most two of Carver, Geography, Kira, and 89%.
If 84% does not go with Chemistry, then either 74% goes with Period 2 or Period 5 goes with Chemistry (but not both).
Period 4 goes with at least two of Nash, Music, and Piper.
The class with Quinn's grade is at least 2% more than the class in Period 4's grade.
Hale goes with at least two of Music, Quinn, 74%, and Period 4.
79%, 89%, and 94% go with Dunn, Hale, and English, in some order.
Either 79% goes with English or Carver goes with English (but not both) if and only if Carver goes with 89%.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: Either Boris goes with 1893 or Mikhail goes with 1793.
Nadia's rating is lower than both the Pirc player's rating and the player who loves the Fork's rating.
Benoni goes with exactly two of Mikhail, Skewer, 1893, and 5th.

Either 1843 goes with London or Magnus goes with London (but not both) if and only if Magnus goes with 1943.
